Tests if the Number object has the "NaN" property.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks if the `Number` object in JavaScript has a property named `NaN`. The `assert` function is used to verify that the `hasOwnProperty` method returns `true`, indicating that `NaN` is indeed a property of the `Number` object. This test is narrow in scope, focusing specifically on the presence of the `NaN` property within the `Number` object. It serves as a validation step to ensure that the JavaScript environment conforms to expected behavior regarding the `Number` object properties.
